sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Mrs. King, is a 37-year-old female presenting with a chief complaint of acid reflux.
She reports a several-year history of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with symptoms of burning sensation in her chest and throat, which have worsened over the past few years.
She has been taking omeprazole 40 mg twice daily for approximately one year, but her symptoms are poorly controlled.
She identifies trigger foods such as spicy foods, carbonated beverages, citrus, and tomato-based foods, which she has not completely avoided.
She also reports high stress levels and anxiety, which she believes exacerbate her symptoms.
She has a history of anxiety and seasonal allergies, and her grandmother had esophageal cancer on her mother's side.
She has not had an upper endoscopy in the past 10 years.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of anxiety and seasonal allergies.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, changes in bowel movements, constipation, diarrhea, or rectal bleeding.
Current medications:
I prescribed omeprazole 40 mg twice daily for GERD.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The physical exam was normal except for a well-healed abdominal surgical scar, which the patient attributes to a surgery she had at one month old due to twisted intestines.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with underlying functional etiology.
Problem list: GERD; Anxiety; Seasonal allergies.
Plan:
I will send a prescription for nexium 40 mg twice daily to her pharmacy on file to replace her current omeprazole.
I recommend that she undergo an esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) to look for potential causes of her GERD, including ulcers, precancerous, or cancerous conditions.
I will also discuss the results of the EGD and her response to the dietary and stress changes, as well as the medication switch, in her follow-up appointment.
I will also consider a trial of protonix and obtain a pH impedance study if her symptoms persist despite a trial of nexium.
Follow-up:
I will schedule an EGD procedure today, and she will follow up with me in the office to discuss the results and her progress.
